questions.question: Which input type restricts the user to entering only a valid email address format?
questions.explanation: The email input type provides built-in validation that checks for a valid email address format (e.g., user@domain.com) before the form can be submitted.

questions.question: What does the 'pattern' attribute on an input element do?
choices: Sets a placeholder text for the input
Specifies a regular expression the input value must match
Sets the maximum length of the input
questions.explanation: The 'pattern' attribute specifies a regular expression that the input's value is checked against during form validation. It works with text, search, url, tel, email, and password input types.

questions.question: Which input type displays a slider control for selecting a numeric value within a range?
questions.explanation: The 'range' input type renders as a slider control, allowing users to select a value between a minimum and maximum. Unlike 'number', it does not show the exact value by default.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'required' attribute on an input element?
choices: It prevents the form from being submitted if the input is empty
It automatically fills in a default value
It disables the input until another field is filled
questions.explanation: The 'required' attribute is a boolean attribute that prevents form submission if the input field is left empty. The browser will display a validation message prompting the user to fill in the field.

questions.question: The input type ___ allows users to pick a color using a color picker widget.
questions.explanation: The 'color' input type displays a color picker that lets users select a color. The value is returned as a hexadecimal color code (e.g., #ff0000).

questions.question: The ___ attribute on an input element displays hint text that disappears when the user starts typing.
questions.explanation: The 'placeholder' attribute provides a short hint describing the expected value of the input field. It is displayed inside the field when it is empty and disappears when the user begins entering text.
